mnemonics: HADOOP: Handles All Data Of Outrageous Proportions
keyPoints: Scalable: Designed to store and process petabytes of data across clusters of commodity hardware.
Cost-Effective: Uses inexpensive, off-the-shelf hardware instead of expensive proprietary systems.
Fault-Tolerant: Automatically handles node failures and data replication to ensure data is never lost.
commonMistakes: Thinking Hadoop is a database (it's a framework for distributed storage and processing).
Believing it is for real-time analytics (it's optimized for batch processing).
